page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r6a.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor and an x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AMD EPYC 7H12 processor clocked at 2.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between compute and memory capabilities. The AMD EPYC processor delivers average performance in PassMark CPU tests, including encryption and extended instructions, alongside average single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, raw compute benchmarks show limitations, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance scoring in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. Memory performance is mixed; cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 400.15 GB/sec, whereas uncached large-block read and write speeds fall into the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high density of RAM relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive CPU performance. Benchmark results show average performance in Redis SET operations (7.13 million operations per second) and static web serving throughput (6.77 GB/sec). Consequently, the ecs.r6a.2xlarge is configured for in-memory caching, database operations, and web serving workloads where memory capacity is the primary constraint.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r6a.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average performance in Redis SET operations, database operations, and static web serving, though raw CPU compute and uncached memory bandwidth fall into lower percentiles. This resource profile supports memory-intensive workloads that require significant RAM capacity but have moderate CPU demands, such as in-memory caching, database hosting, and general web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r6a.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for database and in-memory caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for caching and database workloads.
